mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during economic contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns (Sharpe < 0.5) over 60-day horizons, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflect supply chain bottlenecks that constrain output and trigger negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 65 percent of post-shock contraction periods, or the mechanism shows no directional bias (Rank IC near zero) outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and force monetary tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in 60 percent of high-inflation observations, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and equity returns remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in 55 percent of high-growth quarters, or the threshold signal produces a Rank IC below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates creates a favorable growth-and-liquidity environment that generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 50 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during late-cycle expansion phases signal peak consumer exhaustion that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60 percent of late-cycle retail sales declines, or the signal fails to distinguish late-cycle downturns from normal cyclical fluctuations (Rank IC < 0.02).
